item.title: At least 3 killed and 5 injured in Dallas explosion, officials say
item.description: The number of victims could change as details continue to unfold and crews search through the debris.

item.title: Judge blocks closure of Kennedy Center, orders removal of Trump's name
item.description: A judge blocked the Kennedy Center from closing its doors during renovations, and ruled that its board acted unlawfully by adding President Trump's name to the building. The president reacted by saying he wants Congress to take it over.

item.title: Annie Farmer says fellow Epstein survivors still have "a lot of questions" after Bondi testimony
item.description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi testified behind closed doors on Friday as a part of the House Oversight Committee's investigation into convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Annie Farmer, an Epstein survivor, reacts to Bondi's testimony.

item.title: Blue Origin searches for answers after rocket explosion
item.description: Blue Origin suffered a significant setback on Thursday night when one of its rockets exploded on the launch pad. The massive fireball could be seen for miles. CBS News space consultant Bill Harwood joins to assess what's next.

item.title: Bondi acknowledges Epstein files "redaction errors" in testimony to House Oversight Committee
item.description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi defended the Justice Department's handling of the Epstein files on Capitol Hill on Friday. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion has the details.

item.title: Democratic socialist Chris Rabb on the significance of his victory in Pennsylvania House primary
item.description: Last week's primary elections likely gave democratic socialists another voice in Congress. Democratic voters in Pennsylvania's Philadelphia-based 3rd Congressional District, the most Democratic district in the nation, nominated Chris Rabb. Rabb joins "The Takeout" to discuss the significance of his victory.

item.title: 5 economic signals suggest U.S. consumers are feeling the strain
item.description: Consumers have kept the economy chugging along despite financial pressures. But some signs suggest they could be losing steam, experts say.

item.title: Ukraine bracing for "big attacks" from Russia, Zelenskyy says
item.description: There are deepening fears of an imminent large-scale Russian attack on Kyiv. CBS News chief foreign affairs correspondent and "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan spoke with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy about the state of the war.

item.title: Trump's Situation Room meeting over Iran ends with no details on status of deal
item.description: President Trump said Friday he was meeting with top advisers to prepare a final determination of the next steps on an agreement with Iran. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes reports.

item.title: Trump holds key White House meeting to determine next steps on Iran deal
item.description: President Trump said Friday that Iran must agree to never have a nuclear weapon and to reopen the Strait or Hormuz immediately, without tolls.

item.title: 3 climbers dead, 1 rescued after fall while climbing America's tallest peak
item.description: One climber was rescued from the 17,200-foot basin on Alaska's Mount McKinley, and the search for three remaining climbers who also fell is now a recovery effort, the National Park Service said.

item.title: ICE temporarily releases man facing deportation to Congo amid Ebola outbreak
item.description: Jose Yugar-Cruz, who had been granted protection from deportation to his home country in South America, has been temporarily released from ICE custody.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 31)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: What's different about this Ebola outbreak?
item.description: The World Health Organization said there are approximately 906 suspected Ebola cases in the Democratic Republic of Congo, and they are investigating 223 suspected deaths. Dr. Tom Frieden, president and CEO of Resolve to Save Lives, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: 5 killed, dozens injured when bus plows into several vehicles in Virginia
item.description: The five deaths came in vehicles that were struck by the bus when it did not slow down for traffic, Virginia State Police said.

item.title: Critics got California's free diaper math wrong, but state won't release key Baby2Baby records
item.description: A CBS News California fact-check found the state's free diaper program won't cost taxpayers 50 cents per diaper, as viral posts claimed. But the Newsom administration still won't release the Baby2Baby contract or competitive bid records amid concerns free diapers could go to waste.

item.title: Why did Trump postpone signing an AI executive order?
item.description: Last week, President Trump was expected to sign an executive order on artificial intelligence, but abruptly postponed the event. Politico White House reporter Diana Nerozzi joins to examine what happened.

item.title: Why have tomato prices surged nearly 40% in a year?
item.description: Experts point to several factors, from tariffs to weather, behind the rapid price increase in the humble tomato.

item.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine bracing for "big attacks" by Russia in next 24 hours
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Ukraine has received intelligence indicating Russia will launch an assault involving drones and missiles.

item.title: White House has yet to release results of Trump's latest physical
item.description: The White House has yet to release a summary the results of President Trump's latest physical exam.

item.title: Romania says Russian drone hit apartments, lightly wounding 2 people
item.description: The Russian drone struck an apartment building, wounding two people, Romanian officials said.

item.title: Blue Origin assesses impact of New Glenn explosion
item.description: Blue Origin assess the impact of Thursday's New Glenn explosion, prompting concern about NASA moon program delays.

item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for May 31, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, Democratic Sen. Chris Murphy and former Vice President Mike Pence.

item.title: No tax charges filed in SPLC probe after IRS lawyers found informant program lawful
item.description: Federal agents from FBI and IRS probed the SPLC's paid informant program starting in Trump's first term.

item.title: Medical expert on prostate cancer as Jill Biden describes Joe Biden's "shocking" diagnosis
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden is commenting on former President Joe Biden's "shocking" prostate diagnosis in an interview for "CBS Sunday Morning." Mount Sinai's Dr. Michael Herman joins with more.

item.title: How Colombian presidential election will impact U.S. relationship
item.description: Colombians are heading to the polls to elect their next president, who is bound to impact relations with the U.S., a key regional ally. María Teresa Aya with Noticias Caracol joins CBS News with more.

item.title: She waited for a soulmate who never showed up: ChatGPT users detail AI delusions
item.description: AI-fueled delusions can happen when chatbots respond to grandiose, paranoid or imaginary ideas with affirmation or encouragement.

item.title: ICE agent accused of shooting man in north Minneapolis arrested in Texas
item.description: An Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent accused of shooting a man in the leg in north Minneapolis and then lying about the attack​ was arrested in Texas Friday morning, according to the Hennepin County Attorney's Office.

item.title: Democrats argue ballroom construction shouldn't continue without Congress' consent
item.description: Democratic lawmakers argue the Trump administration must get express consent from Congress before continuing construction on the White House ballroom.

item.title: Man facing ICE deportation to Congo: "I feel like a person who has no value"
item.description: Jose Yugar-Cruz was granted a court order preventing his deportation to his home country, but the Trump administration is set to send him to Congo.
